key_learnings: Embodied cognition formalizes what McGilchrist describes phenomenologically — enactivism provides mechanistic account of sensorimotor engagement producing meaning; McGilchrist provides hemispheric neuroscience grounding for why two distinct modes exist at all

Three failure modes map across frameworks: freezing fluid experience → schema rigidity; bypassing embodiment → Emissary Rebellion during right-mode offline windows; metric replacement → IDI drift where system optimizes for stated goals but loses operator intent alignment

Ambient awareness as baseline: always-on visual/presence channels provide right-hemisphere grounding during left-dominant cadenced work — not 'nice to have' but architectural necessity preventing Emissary Rebellion

Operational closure verification needed: regular audits checking coordination tools remain subordinate to human meaning-making, with mechanisms for detecting metric replacement and abstraction pathology
